skill.name: security-audit
skill.description: Review command execution, tool permissions, secrets handling, WebSocket security, frontend risks, and deployment readiness.
skill.summary: Run a read-only security review before deployment.
skill.author: AppleLamps

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged backtick code spans, dotenv text, environment file text, and API key wording in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are audit checklist items, not executable code, secret access, cryptography use, or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Teams need a focused security review before deployment. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through read-only checks for commands, tools, secrets, WebSockets, and frontend risks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews terminal command handling for shell injection and timeout controls.
Checks tool permission boundaries for chat and agent modes.
Guides review of API key handling and dotenv usage.
Examines WebSocket authentication, validation, and input injection risks.
Reviews frontend risks such as markdown XSS and client-side secret exposure.
Produces a security report with critical issues, warnings, and recommendations.
limitations: The skill is a checklist and does not include automated scanners.
It is limited to read-only review and does not apply fixes directly.
Findings depend on the files available to the assistant.
It does not replace professional penetration testing or compliance review.

use_cases.title: Pre-Deployment Security Review
use_cases.description: Run a targeted review before production release to find command, secret, and frontend risks.

use_cases.title: AI Tool Permission Audit
use_cases.description: Check that chat and agent modes expose only the expected tools and permissions.

use_cases.title: Web Application Risk Triage
use_cases.description: Review WebSocket, markdown, upload, and client-side data handling risks in one workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Security Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a read-only security audit and summarize critical issues, warnings, and recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before sharing a prototype.

prompt_templates.title: Deployment Readiness Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project for command injection, tool permission, API key, WebSocket, and frontend security risks before deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Release review for a web application.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Secrets Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Check for hardcoded API keys, unsafe dotenv handling, and sensitive data exposure. Do not print any discovered secret values.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Threat Model Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit command execution, permission boundaries, WebSocket authentication, message validation, markdown rendering, uploads, and client-side data exposure. Prioritize exploitable findings.

output_examples.input: Run a security audit before deploy.
output_examples.output: The report lists critical issues first, then warnings and recommendations. It covers command execution, tool permissions, secrets, WebSocket checks, and frontend risks.

output_examples.input: Check if our API key handling is safe.
output_examples.output: The review identifies whether keys are hardcoded, whether dotenv usage is appropriate, and whether secret values could appear in logs or client code.
The response checks authentication, message validation, and user input handling. It highlights injection points and deployment blockers.

best_practices: Run the audit before production deployment and after major permission changes.
Provide access to backend, frontend, and configuration files for complete coverage.
Keep secret values redacted when sharing findings or logs.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the assistant to exploit vulnerabilities during the review.
Do not paste raw production secrets into the prompt.
Do not treat the checklist as a substitute for automated testing and human review.

faq.question: Does this skill modify files?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructions define a read-only audit and require approval before any fixes.

faq.question: Can it find command injection risks?
faq.answer: Yes. It directs the assistant to inspect terminal command handling and dangerous command patterns.

faq.question: Does it expose secrets during review?
faq.answer: No. The guardrails tell the assistant to never log or expose discovered secrets.

faq.question: Which tools are requested by the skill?
faq.answer: The skill requests read-only file inspection tools: Read, Grep, and Glob.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code support.

faq.question: Is this enough for compliance certification?
faq.answer: No. It supports security triage, but formal compliance requires separate evidence and expert review.
